slides.title: The tiny sprout that *flips a switch* inside you
slides.sub: Sulforaphane from broccoli sprouts can wake up your body's own defense system. Here is how it works.

slides.title: Meet sulforaphane
slides.body: Broccoli sprouts are unusually rich in a compound called *sulforaphane*. Gram for gram, they carry far more than the mature vegetable.

slides.title: It activates Nrf2
slides.body: Sulforaphane turns on *Nrf2*, a master regulator that tells your cells to ramp up their own antioxidant and detox defenses.

slides.title: Your enzymes do the work
slides.body: Instead of one dose of antioxidant, Nrf2 *switches on enzymes* that keep working for hours, defending cells from the inside.

slides.title: Calmer oxidative stress
slides.body: Oxidative stress is linked to faster aging. Blunting it *may help* protect cells, tissues and the way you feel over time.

slides.title: Small, steady, repeatable
slides.body: A handful of broccoli sprouts on a salad or bowl is enough. The win comes from *making it a habit*, not a single meal.

slides.title: Turn good habits into *one number*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your food, sleep, movement and steps, then turns the small daily choices into one Health Score you can actually follow.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A tiny broccoli sprout can switch on your body's own antioxidant defenses for hours. 🌿✨

Sulforaphane activates the Nrf2 pathway, your enzymes do the work, and steady oxidative stress is what may help you age slower. Save this for your next salad. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those small daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Broccoli Sprouts and Nrf2: The Longevity Switch
